generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-based occupancy sensor embedded in physical building spaces, integrated with BIM semantics for real-time occupancy detection. Used in BOREALIS for adaptive control policy refinement."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyTelemetry
]

instance LightLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-based light sensor measuring ambient and artificial lighting levels, integrated with TwinLight project for lighting control automation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightLevelTelemetry
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Automation System (BAS) temperature sensor providing real-time thermal data for HVAC and energy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureTelemetry
]

instance EnergyMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy consumption meter tracking real-time power usage across building zones, integrated with BIM for spatial energy analytics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionTelemetry
]

instance PostOccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Specialized sensor capturing post-occupancy data for iterative refinement of reinforcement learning control policies in BOREALIS project."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PostOccupancyData
]

instance BASControlFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Automation System sensor providing control system feedback including actuator states and system alerts."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BASControlFeedback
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy data transmitted via Eclipse Hono using MQTT protocol, normalized before ingestion into Eclipse Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance LightLevelTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Light level measurements transmitted from IoT sensors to Eclipse Hono, processed for TwinLight lighting control logic."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightLevelSensor
]

instance TemperatureTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Thermal data stream from BAS sensors, transmitted via AMQP to Eclipse Hono for real-time building monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Energy consumption metrics transmitted from building zones, used for real-time energy optimization scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyMeter
]

instance PostOccupancyData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy sensor data collected for iterative refinement of BOREALIS reinforcement learning policies."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PostOccupancySensor
]

instance BASControlFeedback : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Control system feedback including actuator states and system alerts transmitted from BAS to Eclipse Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BASControlFeedbackSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMEnrichedSemanticModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM model enriched with runtime semantics including sensor/actuator metadata, control logic, and spatial relationships for operational data integration."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMToRuntimeTransformer
]

instance RuntimeControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Control logic definitions generated via model transformations, including TwinLight lighting strategies and BOREALIS reinforcement learning models."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicDeployer
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D visualization assets generated from BIM model for immersive monitoring in Unreal Engine 5, including interactive building system representations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationRenderer
]

instance RealTimeSensorTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time sensor data from all physical sensing components, normalized and stored in Eclipse Ditto for processing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyTelemetry, LightLevelTelemetry, TemperatureTelemetry, EnergyConsumptionTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoDataProcessor
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Archived operational data including sensor telemetry, control logs, and analytics outputs for trend analysis and long-term optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PostOccupancyData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HistoricalAnalyticsService
]

instance DeviceConfigurationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Metadata extracted from BIM model including device configurations, spatial relationships, and middleware-specific attributes for Eclipse Hono/Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BASControlFeedback
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DeviceRegistrationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "IoT middleware for real-time data ingestion from heterogeneous sensor networks using MQTT/AMQP protocols with policy-based access control."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceRegistrationService
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin management system providing twin lifecycle management, bidirectional synchronization, and standardized JSON-based twin descriptions."
    DTDFVocab:enables DittoDataProcessor
]

instance UnrealEngine5 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D visualization platform enabling real-time rendering and physics-based interactions for building system monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationRenderer
]

instance ReinforcementLearningFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning framework used in BOREALIS project for adaptive control policy optimization through sensor feedback loops."
    DTDFVocab:enables PolicyRefinementService
]

instance BIMToRuntimeTransformer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven engineering tool performing model-to-model and model-to-text transformations from BIM to runtime artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceConfigurationGenerator, ControlLogicDeployer
]

instance DeviceConfigurationGenerator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Generates Eclipse Hono/Ditto configurations including device registrations and policy rules from enriched BIM semantics."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceRegistrationService
]

instance ControlLogicDeployer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Deploys control logic definitions (e.g., TwinLight lighting strategies) to the digital twin runtime environment."
]

instance VisualizationRenderer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Renders 3D visualization assets in Unreal Engine 5 with interactive controls for facility managers."
]

instance DittoDataProcessor : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processes and validates real-time sensor data before ingestion into Eclipse Ditto's digital twin management system."
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time visualization of building operations through aggregated sensor telemetry and system status updates."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingOperationalViewInsight, SystemHealthMetricsInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Uses reinforcement learning to dynamically adjust lighting and energy systems for optimal performance balancing energy consumption and visual comfort."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationRecommendationAction, VisualComfortAdjustmentAction
]

instance AutomatedControlConfigurationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables BIM-centric control logic definition and automated deployment of control strategies like occupancy-based lighting."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlLogicValidationInsight, AutomatedCommissioningAction
]

instance SimulationAndScenarioTestingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supports what-if analyses for operational adjustments including energy-saving scenarios before physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioOutcomeInsight, OptimizationScenarioRecommendationAction
]

instance HistoricalAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Analyzes archived operational data for trend identification, validation, and long-term optimization of building systems."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyConsumptionTrendInsight, OccupancyPatternInsight
]

instance InteractiveMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides spatially aware 3D visualization of building operations with interactive controls for facility managers."
    DTDFVocab:provides FacilityManagerDashboardInsight, InteractiveControlAction
]

instance DeviceRegistrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Configures Eclipse Hono with device registrations and policy rules extracted from BIM semantics."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceRegistrationConfirmationInsight
]

instance PolicyRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Iteratively refines control policies using post-occupancy sensor data and reinforcement learning feedback."
    DTDFVocab:provides PolicyImprovementInsight, AdaptiveControlAdjustmentAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingOperationalViewInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of building operations including sensor telemetry and system status across all zones."
]

instance SystemHealthMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive metrics for sensor/actuator health including failure predictions and maintenance requirements."
]

instance EnergyOptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning-derived recommendations for energy-efficient HVAC and lighting setpoints."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VisualComfortAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustments to lighting systems balancing visual comfort and energy consumption."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlLogicValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation metrics ensuring alignment between digital twin control logic and physical building systems."
]

instance AutomatedCommissioningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated deployment of control strategies directly from BIM models without manual configuration."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ScenarioOutcomeInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted outcomes of what-if scenarios including energy consumption impacts and operational adjustments."
]

instance OptimizationScenarioRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommended operational adjustments based on simulation results before physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EnergyConsumptionTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical and real-time trends in energy consumption identifying peak usage and waste patterns."
]

instance OccupancyPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analyzed occupancy patterns including underutilized spaces and peak activity periods."
]

instance FacilityManagerDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Customizable dashboard for facility managers with real-time operational views and interactive controls."
]

instance InteractiveControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual override capabilities for facility managers to adjust lighting zones and other systems via Unreal Engine interface."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DeviceRegistrationConfirmationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Confirmation of successful device registration in Eclipse Hono with associated policy rules."
]

instance PolicyImprovementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Metrics showing iterative improvements in control policies based on post-occupancy data analysis."
]

instance AdaptiveControlAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of control parameters based on reinforcement learning feedback loops."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control system actuator for lighting fixtures integrated with TwinLight project for automated control."
]

instance HVACDamperActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC system actuator controlling airflow and temperature regulation in building zones."
]

instance ShadingSystemActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Adaptive shading system actuator for dynamic control of natural light and thermal comfort."
]

instance IoTSmartActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled actuator integrating with reinforcement learning systems for adaptive building control in BOREALIS project."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The interactions from the virtual world to the physical world are enabled through **bidirectional communication channels** established via **Eclipse Hono and Ditto**, allowing the DT to send control signals back to the Physical Twin (PT). Key outputs include:
  - **Control commands** (e.g., lighting dimming levels, HVAC setpoints) generated by the DT’s optimization algorithms (e.g., reinforcement learning in BOREALIS or rule-based logic in TwinLight).
  - **Automated commissioning directives** for building control systems, ensuring alignment between virtual and physical configurations.
  - **Alerts and notifications** triggered by DT analytics (e.g., energy inefficiencies, occupancy anomalies) to facilitate proactive maintenance.
  The framework ensures low-latency synchronization to maintain operational coherence between the DT and PT."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time-scale is **real-time** for operational synchronization, with the following characteristics:
  - **Continuous data synchronization**: Sensor data and control signals are exchanged in near real-time (latency < 1s) via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto**, ensuring the DT reflects the current state of the PT.
  - **Dynamic adaptation**: The DT evolves over time through **iterative reinforcement learning (BOREALIS)**, where control policies are refined based on post-occupancy data, typically updated on a **daily or sub-daily** basis.
  - **Event-driven updates**: Critical events (e.g., sensor failures, occupancy changes) trigger immediate DT updates to maintain fidelity.
  The framework supports **asynchronous processing** for non-critical services (e.g., long-term energy trend analysis) while prioritizing real-time interactions for operational control."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented architecture** with the following components:
  - **BIM Model Repository**: Central semantic backbone storing **static and runtime data** (e.g., geometry, device configurations, control logic).
  - **Middleware Layer**: Comprising **Eclipse Hono** (for IoT data) and **Eclipse Ditto** (for twin management), enabling **real-time bidirectional communication**.
  - **Control Logic Layer**: Hosts **automated control strategies** (e.g., TwinLight’s lighting rules, BOREALIS’s RL policies) and **custom services** integrated via extension points.
  - **Visualization Layer**: **Unreal Engine 5** provides **interactive 3D dashboards** for facility managers, with **real-time data binding** to the DT.
  - **Extension Points**: Standardized interfaces for **third-party services** (e.g., predictive maintenance, energy analytics) to plug into the DT ecosystem.
  The constellation supports **modular scaling**, allowing the DT to grow from **single-building deployments** to **multi-building or campus-wide systems** while maintaining interoperability."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans the following lifecycle phases of the physical building, with corresponding representation phases:
  - **Design phase**: The DT is initialized from the **BIM model**, incorporating static design data (geometry, spatial relationships, component properties).
  - **Construction phase**: The DT is **enriched with device metadata** (e.g., sensor/actuator configurations) and validated against the **as-built model** to ensure alignment.
  - **Commissioning phase**: The DT undergoes **automated deployment** via the model-driven framework, establishing bidirectional communication with the PT (e.g., TwinLight’s control configuration).
  - **Operational phase**: The DT operates in **real-time**, supporting **continuous monitoring, adaptive control (BOREALIS), and predictive maintenance**. The DT evolves through **runtime updates** (e.g., reinforcement learning policy refinements) without requiring full redeployment.
  - **Refurbishment/Adaptation phase**: The DT accommodates **changes in the physical infrastructure** (e.g., retrofitting, reconfiguration) by updating the BIM model and regenerating runtime artifacts, ensuring long-term compatibility."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are addressed through:
  - **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**: Quantifies the **alignment between the DT and PT** by evaluating:
    - **Data synchronization accuracy** (e.g., sensor data consistency).
    - **Control logic fidelity** (e.g., adherence of physical actuators to virtual commands).
    - **Temporal coherence** (e.g., latency in real-time updates).
  - **Validation mechanisms**:
    - **Automated model validation** (e.g., checking BIM model consistency before deployment).
    - **Runtime monitoring** of Hono/Ditto for data integrity and protocol compliance.
    - **User feedback loops** (e.g., facility managers validating DT outputs against physical observations).
  - **Uncertainty management**:
    - **Sensor calibration** and **data normalization** to mitigate measurement errors.
    - **Fallback mechanisms** for failed communications (e.g., gracefully degrading control logic if sensor data is unavailable).
  - **Error handling**: The framework includes **retry policies** for failed operations and **audit logs** for post-mortem analysis."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the Physical Twin (PT) and Digital Twin (DT) is established via:
  - **Network protocols**:
    - **MQTT/AMQP** (via Eclipse Hono) for **lightweight, publish-subscribe IoT communication**.
    - **REST APIs** for **service-to-service interactions** (e.g., between custom plugins and the DT core).
    - **WebSockets** for **real-time visualization updates** in Unreal Engine.
  - **Architecture**:
    - **Hybrid cloud-edge deployment**: Sensor data is ingested at the **edge** (e.g., on-premise gateways) and aggregated in the **cloud** (e.g., Hono/Ditto servers) for centralized processing.
    - **Standardized data models**: Eclipse Ditto enforces **JSON-based twin descriptions** for interoperability.
  - **Security**:
    - **TLS encryption** for all communications.
    - **Policy-based access control** in Hono/Ditto to restrict unauthorized modifications."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted and deployed using:
  - **Middleware infrastructure**:
    - **Eclipse Hono**: Deployed as a **scalable IoT middleware** (e.g., Kubernetes clusters) to handle high-throughput sensor data.
    - **Eclipse Ditto**: Runs as a **digital twin management service** (e.g., cloud-based or on-premise) to manage twin lifecycles and synchronization.
  - **Visualization platform**:
    - **Unreal Engine 5**: Hosted on **high-performance workstations** or **cloud-based rendering farms** for interactive 3D experiences.
  - **Deployment model**:
    - **Containerized microservices** (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) for modular components (e.g., control logic, analytics).
    - **Automated CI/CD pipelines** to regenerate runtime artifacts from BIM model updates.
  - **Scalability**:
    - Supports **horizontal scaling** for large buildings or campuses by partitioning the DT into **sub-twins** (e.g., per-floor or per-zone).
    - **Edge computing** for latency-sensitive services (e.g., local control logic execution)."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration with external systems is enabled through:
  - **Standardized data exchange**:
    - **BIM export/import** (e.g., IFC, BCF formats) for interoperability with other **AECO tools** (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD).
    - **IoT platform integration** (e.g., connecting to **Siemens Desigo, Honeywell BMS**) via **Hono’s protocol adapters**.
  - **API gateways**:
    - **RESTful APIs** for **third-party service integration** (e.g., ERP systems, CMMS).
    - **Event-driven architectures** (e.g., Kafka topics) for **asynchronous data sharing** with external DTs or enterprise systems.
  - **Semantic interoperability**:
    - **Ontology-based mappings** to align DT data models with **external standards** (e.g., OPC UA for industrial IoT).
    - **Common Data Environment (CDE)** integration for **collaborative AECO workflows**."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy are addressed through:
  - **Role-based access control (RBAC)**:
    - **BIM model ownership**: Retained by the **building owner** or **design authority**.
    - **Operational data ownership**: Shared between **facility managers** and **DT service providers**, with **tenant-specific access policies** in Hono/Ditto.
  - **Privacy-preserving mechanisms**:
    - **Data anonymization** for occupancy/sensor data in public spaces.
    - **Differential privacy** for analytics to prevent re-identification.
  - **Compliance frameworks**:
    - **GDPR alignment** for personal data (e.g., occupancy logs).
    - **Industry-specific standards** (e.g., ISO 55000 for asset management).
  - **Audit trails**: All data access and modifications are logged for **transparency and accountability**."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards:
  - **BIM standards**:
    - **Industry Foundation Classes (IFC)** for semantic interoperability.
    - **BuildingSMART Data Dictionary (bSDD)** for consistent terminology.
  - **IoT and middleware standards**:
    - **OASIS Context Broker Architecture (O-CBA)** for Eclipse Hono’s interoperability.
    - **Eclipse Ditto’s Digital Twin Definition Language (DTDL)** for twin descriptions.
  - **Control systems standards**:
    - **BACnet** or **LonWorks** for building automation integration (where applicable).
    - **IEC 61131-3** for programmable logic controllers (PLCs) in TwinLight/BOREALIS.
  - **Visualization standards**:
    - **OpenGL/Vulkan** (via Unreal Engine) for cross-platform rendering.
    - **WebXR** for **AR/VR interoperability** in collaborative scenarios.
  - **Security standards**:
    - **ISO/IEC 27001** for information security management.
    - **NIST SP 800-53** for system and communication protection."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  - **Cybersecurity measures**:
    - **End-to-end encryption** (TLS 1.3) for all communications between PT and DT.
    - **Zero-trust architecture** in Hono/Ditto, requiring **mutual authentication** for device onboarding.
    - **Intrusion detection systems (IDS)** monitoring for anomalous behavior in control signals.
  - **Safety mechanisms**:
    - **Fail-safe defaults**: If the DT loses connection to the PT, **predefined safe states** (e.g., emergency lighting on) are enforced.
    - **Redundancy**: Critical control logic is **replicated across nodes** to prevent single points of failure.
    - **Human-in-the-loop validation**: Facility managers can **override automated controls** in Unreal Engine for manual intervention.
  - **Operational safety**:
    - **Real-time monitoring** of **physical constraints** (e.g., actuator limits, environmental thresholds) to prevent unsafe commands.
    - **Audit logs** for **post-incident analysis** (e.g., tracing the cause of a control failure).
  - **Compliance**:
    - **ISO 26262** for functional safety in control systems (where applicable).
    - **IEC 61508** for safety instrumented systems in critical infrastructure."
]
